speaker: LEAR
text: Blow winds, and crack your cheeks! Rage, blow!

speaker: LEAR
text: You cataracts and hurricanoes, spout

speaker: LEAR
text: Till you have drenched our steeples, drowned the cocks.

speaker: LEAR
text: You sulph’rous and thought-executing fires,

speaker: LEAR
text: Vaunt-couriers of oak-cleaving thunderbolts,

speaker: LEAR
text: Singe my white head. And thou, all-shaking thunder,

speaker: LEAR
text: Strike flat the thick rotundity o’ th’ world.

speaker: LEAR
text: Crack nature’s molds, all germens spill at once

speaker: LEAR
text: That makes ingrateful man.

speaker: FOOL
text: O nuncle, court holy water in a dry house is

speaker: FOOL
text: better than this rainwater out o’ door. Good nuncle,

speaker: FOOL
text: in. Ask thy daughters’ blessing. Here’s a night

speaker: FOOL
text: pities neither wise men nor fools.

speaker: LEAR
text: Rumble thy bellyful! Spit, fire! Spout, rain!

speaker: LEAR
text: Nor rain, wind, thunder, fire are my daughters.

speaker: LEAR
text: I tax not you, you elements, with unkindness.

speaker: LEAR
text: I never gave you kingdom, called you children;

speaker: LEAR
text: You owe me no subscription. Then let fall

speaker: LEAR
text: Your horrible pleasure. Here I stand your slave,

speaker: LEAR
text: A poor, infirm, weak, and despised old man.

speaker: LEAR
text: But yet I call you servile ministers,

speaker: LEAR
text: That will with two pernicious daughters join

speaker: LEAR
text: Your high-engendered battles ’gainst a head

speaker: LEAR
text: So old and white as this. O, ho, ’tis foul!

speaker: FOOL
text: He that has a house to put ’s head in has a good

speaker: FOOL
text: headpiece.

speaker: FOOL
text: The codpiece that will house

speaker: FOOL
text: Before the head has any,

speaker: FOOL
text: The head and he shall louse;

speaker: FOOL
text: So beggars marry many.

speaker: FOOL
text: The man that makes his toe

speaker: FOOL
text: What he his heart should make,

speaker: FOOL
text: Shall of a corn cry woe,

speaker: FOOL
text: And turn his sleep to wake.

speaker: FOOL
text: For there was never yet fair woman but she made

speaker: FOOL
text: mouths in a glass.

speaker: LEAR
text: No, I will be the pattern of all patience.

speaker: LEAR
text: I will say nothing.

speaker: KENT
text: Who’s there?

speaker: FOOL
text: Marry, here’s grace and a codpiece; that’s a

speaker: FOOL
text: wise man and a fool.

speaker: KENT
text: Alas, sir, are you here? Things that love night

speaker: KENT
text: Love not such nights as these. The wrathful skies

speaker: KENT
text: Gallow the very wanderers of the dark

speaker: KENT
text: And make them keep their caves. Since I was man,

speaker: KENT
text: Such sheets of fire, such bursts of horrid thunder,

speaker: KENT
text: Such groans of roaring wind and rain I never

speaker: KENT
text: Remember to have heard. Man’s nature cannot carry

speaker: KENT
text: Th’ affliction nor the fear.

speaker: LEAR
text: Let the great gods

speaker: LEAR
text: That keep this dreadful pudder o’er our heads

speaker: LEAR
text: Find out their enemies now. Tremble, thou wretch,

speaker: LEAR
text: That hast within thee undivulgèd crimes

speaker: LEAR
text: Unwhipped of justice. Hide thee, thou bloody hand,

speaker: LEAR
text: Thou perjured, and thou simular of virtue

speaker: LEAR
text: That art incestuous. Caitiff, to pieces shake,

speaker: LEAR
text: That under covert and convenient seeming

speaker: LEAR
text: Has practiced on man’s life. Close pent-up guilts,

speaker: LEAR
text: Rive your concealing continents and cry

speaker: LEAR
text: These dreadful summoners grace. I am a man

speaker: LEAR
text: More sinned against than sinning.

speaker: KENT
text: Alack, bareheaded?

speaker: KENT
text: Gracious my lord, hard by here is a hovel.

speaker: KENT
text: Some friendship will it lend you ’gainst the tempest.

speaker: KENT
text: Repose you there while I to this hard house—

speaker: KENT
text: More harder than the stones whereof ’tis raised,

speaker: KENT
text: Which even but now, demanding after you,

speaker: KENT
text: Denied me to come in—return and force

speaker: KENT
text: Their scanted courtesy.

speaker: LEAR
text: My wits begin to turn.—

speaker: LEAR
text: Come on, my boy. How dost, my boy? Art cold?

speaker: LEAR
text: I am cold myself.—Where is this straw, my fellow?

speaker: LEAR
text: The art of our necessities is strange

speaker: LEAR
text: And can make vile things precious. Come, your hovel.—

speaker: LEAR
text: Poor Fool and knave, I have one part in my heart

speaker: LEAR
text: That’s sorry yet for thee.

speaker: FOOL
text: He that has and a little tiny wit,

speaker: FOOL
text: With hey, ho, the wind and the rain,

speaker: FOOL
text: Must make content with his fortunes fit,

speaker: FOOL
text: Though the rain it raineth every day.

speaker: LEAR
text: True, my good boy.—Come, bring us to this hovel.

speaker: FOOL
text: This is a brave night to cool a courtesan. I’ll

speaker: FOOL
text: speak a prophecy ere I go:

speaker: FOOL
text: When priests are more in word than matter,

speaker: FOOL
text: When brewers mar their malt with water,

speaker: FOOL
text: When nobles are their tailors’ tutors,

speaker: FOOL
text: No heretics burned but wenches’ suitors,

speaker: FOOL
text: When every case in law is right,

speaker: FOOL
text: No squire in debt, nor no poor knight;

speaker: FOOL
text: When slanders do not live in tongues,

speaker: FOOL
text: Nor cutpurses come not to throngs,

speaker: FOOL
text: When usurers tell their gold i’ th’ field,

speaker: FOOL
text: And bawds and whores do churches build,

speaker: FOOL
text: Then shall the realm of Albion

speaker: FOOL
text: Come to great confusion;

speaker: FOOL
text: Then comes the time, who lives to see ’t,

speaker: FOOL
text: That going shall be used with feet.

speaker: FOOL
text: This prophecy Merlin shall make, for I live before

speaker: FOOL
text: his time.
